name: The PRIMETIME benchmark.
description: The PRIMETIME benchmark introduces Translate, Compute and Event Planning tasks that aim to measure an LLM's ability on primitive datetime operations.
citeAs: @misc{primetime2026, author       = {Anonymous}, title        = {{PRIMETIME}: A Benchmark for Temporal Translation and Reasoning in Large Language Models}, year         = {2026}, note         = {Under double-blind review at NeurIPS 2026 Datasets and Benchmarks Track} }
rai:annotationsPerItem: One target per item, produced by the deterministic reference implementation.
rai:dataAnnotationAnalysis: Not applicable. Determinism of the reference implementations removes inter-annotator variance.
rai:dataAnnotationProtocol: Targets are produced programmatically by reference implementations of each task (translation to ISO-8601, arithmetic, event-planning composition). No human annotation is involved at any stage.
rai:dataBiases: Language bias: month and weekday names are rendered in English.
Calendar bias: only the proleptic Gregorian calendar is represented.
Format bias: the renderer favours commonly observed Western datetime conventions; non-Western or domain-specific conventions are under-represented.
rai:dataCollection: The benchmark is fully synthetic. Inputs are datetimes sampled uniformly across a wide year range and rendered into natural-language forms via a parameterised generator. Targets are computed deterministically from the inputs by reference implementations, ensuring exact ground truth without human labelling.
rai:dataCollectionMissingData: Not applicable. The synthetic generation pipeline emits complete observations or rejects them at source; no observations contain missing fields.
rai:dataCollectionRawData: No raw source data. All observations are produced by a deterministic generator from random seeds; the generator code is released alongside the dataset.
rai:dataManipulationProtocol: Not applicable. Observations are emitted directly from the generator and are not subsequently transformed.
rai:dataLimitations: The benchmark uses English-language month and weekday names and Western calendar conventions; performance on this benchmark does not necessarily generalise to other locales or calendar systems.
Surface-form coverage is bounded by the renderer; idiosyncratic real-world datetime expressions may fall outside the distribution.
Targets are exact by construction, so the benchmark does not capture the inherent ambiguity present in some real-world datetime references.
rai:dataPreprocessingProtocol: Sampling: uniform sampling over the configured year, month, day, and time-of-day ranges per task.
Rendering: each datetime is rendered into one of several natural-language surface forms (varying separators, month name casings, ordinal suffixes, and timezone presentations).
Validation: each generated observation is round-tripped through the reference implementation to verify ground-truth correctness before inclusion.
rai:dataReleaseMaintenancePlan: Released under semantic versioning. The dataset, generator, and reference implementations are maintained at the public repository linked in the citation. Issues and corrections are tracked via the repository issue tracker; new releases follow patch / minor / major conventions.
rai:dataSocialImpact: By providing a precise, reproducible measure of LLM temporal reasoning, the benchmark supports more rigorous evaluation of models used in scheduling, logging, financial reporting, and other temporally sensitive applications.
rai:dataUseCases: Evaluation of large-language-model temporal-translation and -reasoning capability.
Diagnostic decomposition of model failure modes across surface form, arithmetic, and compositional sub-skills.
Targeted fine-tuning data for improving temporal competence in small models.
rai:machineAnnotationTools: Python 3.12 datetime generator (released with the benchmark)
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: None. The benchmark contains no personal data, no identifiable information, and no content derived from human subjects.

distribution.name: github-repository
distribution.description: Repository of PRIMETIME benchmarks and datasets on GitHub.

distribution.name: PRIMETIME/Translate
distribution.description: Translate the full datetime to ISO-8601 representation without timezone and without microseconds

field.name: input
field.description: A datetime in natural form, e.g 11th.february.5951 ,1:12:31.446879 +0100

field.name: target
field.description: The datetime in ISO-8601 format, without timezone and without microseconds
